articles.title: Auditors and regulators must work in tandem to strengthen Financial reporting ecosystem: NFRA chief Nitin Gupta
articles.description: National Financial Reporting Authority (NFRA) Chairman Nitin Gupta emphasized the need for closer collaboration between regulators and the accounting profession to strengthen audit quality and financial reporting in India. Speaking at an ICAI-NFRA workshop in Pune, he highlighted auditors' expanding responsibilities amid digital transformation and AI adoption. Gupta stressed that strong corporate governance, reliable financial reporting, and credible audits are essential for sustaining India's economic growth. ICAI leaders also underscored continuous professional development and technology-driven skill enhancement.

articles.title: One of Canada's biggest bank, Toronto-Dominion Bank, tells some employees what led to almost 'staff revolt' in Meta
articles.description: Toronto-Dominion Bank is reportedly implementing software to monitor employee activity on work applications, sparking privacy concerns. Similar to Meta's recent initiative, the bank claims the tool, WorkiQ, aims to identify workflow inefficiencies and operational bottlenecks, not to spy on content. Employees are questioning its use in performance evaluations and seeking clarity on consent, while TD assures privacy safeguards are in place.

articles.title: Debit operations of 3 bank accounts of TMC holding Rs 440 cr frozen
articles.description: Kolkata's Trinamool Congress faces a financial crisis as three party accounts, holding approximately Rs 440 crore, have been frozen. Rebel MLAs lodged complaints with cyber crime police, questioning the source of these funds and demanding a probe into alleged illegal activities. This move escalates the internal power struggle within the party, with factions led by Aroop Biswas and Ritabrata Banerjee vying for control.

articles.title: RBI Repo Rate Changes Can Impact Your Home Loan EMI, Here’s How To Stay Prepared
articles.description:
Changes in the repo rate can have a direct impact on home loan borrowers, particularly those with floating-rate or Repo Linked Lending Rate (RLLR) loans. Any revision in the Reserve Bank of India’s benchmark rate can alter monthly instalments, making it important for borrowers to stay alert and plan repayments carefully.

For many homeowners, a change in the repo rate often translates into higher or lower EMIs. As lending rates adjust in response to monetary policy decisions, borrowers who actively track these developments can better prepare for the financial impact.

Experts suggest that a proactive approach towards loan management can help borrowers minimise uncertainty and maintain better control over their finances. Monitoring policy decisions, reviewing repayment plans and exploring available lending options remain crucial steps.

Keep A Close Watch On RBI Policy Decisions

Borrowers should regularly monitor repo rate announcements and remain informed about the effective dates of any changes announced by the Reserve Bank of India. Timely awareness allows borrowers to anticipate potential increases or reductions in their home loan EMIs.

Keeping track of policy decisions also helps borrowers prepare their monthly budgets accordingly. A rate cut or hike can significantly influence repayment obligations, especially for those with floating-rate home loans.

Also Read: NSE IPO Buzz To BharatNet Order: These 3 Stocks Surged Up To 25% In Just One Week

Review Your EMI And Loan Tenure

Every change in lending rates offers borrowers an opportunity to reassess their repayment strategy. Homeowners can evaluate whether they want to continue with the existing EMI amount or make adjustments based on their financial circumstances.

Reviewing the EMI structure can help borrowers optimise repayments. In some cases, maintaining higher EMIs may reduce the overall loan tenure and lower the total interest burden over the life of the loan.

Use Prepayments To Reduce Interest Burden

Periods of lower repo rates may provide borrowers with an opportunity to make additional payments towards the principal amount. Strategic prepayments can reduce the outstanding balance and help cut the overall interest outgo.

Reducing the principal through extra payments can strengthen long-term savings and improve loan repayment efficiency. Even small prepayments made consistently can contribute to lowering the total cost of borrowing.

Also Read: Filing Income Tax This Year? Here's How People Are Using Claude To File Their Own ITR

Speak To Your Lender About Available Options

Borrowers should also discuss repayment options with their lenders whenever interest rates change. Some financial institutions offer flexibility through partial switches between floating and fixed interest rates.

Lenders may also provide repayment alternatives that can reduce the impact of repo rate volatility. Understanding these options allows borrowers to make informed decisions that align with their financial goals.

By following these measures, home loan borrowers can manage changes in repo rates more effectively, maintain greater predictability in their EMIs and make informed financial decisions in response to changing monetary policy conditions.

articles.title: 'WE NEED PLOT TWISTS': Trump Nearly Fired Hegseth In Wild White House Power Drama? Shock Reveal
articles.description: A new report from The New York Times reveals just how close Pete Hegseth may have come to losing his shot at leading the Pentagon. According to the report, President Donald Trump seriously considered replacing Hegseth as Defense Secretary nominee amid mounting scrutiny and political backlash. Florida Governor Ron DeSantis reportedly emerged as a potential alternative as doubts grew over Hegseth's confirmation prospects. In a striking detail, Trump allegedly told aides, "We need plot twists," while discussions unfolded behind closed doors. Despite the uncertainty, Trump ultimately stood by Hegseth, working behind the scenes to save the nomination.

articles.title: World Bank clears $1.5 bn package for India's reforms
articles.description: The World Bank has approved a $1.5 billion financing package for India, aimed at accelerating structural reforms and stimulating private sector-led job creation. This initiative supports economic growth by fostering entrepreneurship and simplifying business processes, building on recent advancements in tax and regulatory frameworks. The funding is expected to create millions of job opportunities for young people entering the workforce annually, reflecting India's progress in governance and inclusive labor empowerment.

articles.title: Dollar firms as US-Iran peace deal hangs in balance; yen nears 40-year low
articles.description: The dollar remains strong against most currencies, nearing a 40-year low against the yen, as a potential US-Iran peace deal hangs in the balance. Federal Reserve projections hinting at a year-end rate hike are boosting the dollar, with traders watching closely for any intervention by Japanese authorities to support their currency. Despite a recent Bank of Japan rate hike, Japanese interest rates remain significantly lower than global counterparts.

articles.title: Delhi HC rules EY US secondment fees taxable in India, MNCs face higher costs for foreign talent
articles.description: Multinational corporations in India face a new tax challenge following a High Court ruling against EY US. The court determined that payments for employees deputed to India constitute taxable 'foreign technical services' (FTS), not mere cost reimbursements. This decision could increase the cost of bringing in foreign expertise by up to 10%, prompting companies to re-evaluate their secondment arrangements and potential GST liabilities.
